title: HTTP Module Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of createServer, req/res objects, and basic routing using the req.url property.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primarily role of the 'http' module in Node.js?
options: To listen for incoming network requests and send back professional, structured responses to your users.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.

questions.question: Which and/or what was the primarily benefit of using 'http.createServer'?
options: It provides a callback that runs every time a user visits your site.
It only works for mobile apps.

questions.question: Which object in the 'createServer' callback contains all the information from the user (URL, headers, query parameters, data)?
Which and/or what object is used to send data back to the user (status codes, headers, and final content)?
Which and/or what property would you use in the 'req' object to built a simple 'router' based on the path the user requested?
